title: Fear No More – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: William Shakespeare

questions.question: Who wrote the poem 'Fear No More'?
questions.explanation: Shakespeare was a famous English playwright and poet.

questions.question: What does the poem mainly talk about?
questions.explanation: The poem suggests that death frees people from worldly troubles.

questions.question: What does the poet say people no longer fear after death?
questions.correct_answer: Heat of the sun and winter’s cold
questions.explanation: These represent the hardships of life.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'come to dust' mean?
questions.explanation: It symbolises the inevitable fate of every person.

questions.question: Which two groups are mentioned to show equality in death?
questions.explanation: Both rich and poor share the same destiny.

questions.question: What fear disappears after death according to the poem?
questions.explanation: Death frees people from social judgement.

questions.question: What does the poem suggest about worldly power?
questions.correct_answer: It has no value after death
questions.explanation: Titles, knowledge, and status all end with death.

questions.question: What tone does the poem use?
questions.explanation: The poem tries to comfort readers about death.

questions.question: What universal truth is presented in the poem?
questions.explanation: The poem emphasises the inevitability of death.

questions.question: What is the main message of the poem?
questions.correct_answer: Death brings peace and equality to all humans
questions.explanation: The poem presents death as the end of suffering and social differences.

notes.title: Equality in Death
notes.content: The poem explains that all people, rich or poor, eventually face the same fate.

notes.title: Peace After Life
notes.content: Death is presented as a peaceful rest after the struggles of life.

facts.fact: The poem emphasises the inevitability of death.
William Shakespeare wrote many famous plays and poems.
